scene.description: Engine 181 is supplying a 250' 2½" attack line to the corner of a commercial strip. The nozzle is a 1⅛" smooth bore flowing about 265 GPM at 50 PSI. No elevation and no appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure should you set?

problems.title: Heavy 2½ Smooth Bore Attack Line
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a 250 ft 2½-inch attack line with a 1⅛-inch smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 GPM at 50 PSI. No elevation and no appliance loss. Use C = 2. What pump discharge pressure should you set?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds of feet.
problems.instructorExplanation: This heavy-line variation uses 250 ft of 2½-inch hose at 265 GPM. Calculate friction loss with C = 2, then add nozzle pressure and elevation.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the same answer for every 2½-inch line problem. Hose length and elevation can change the final pump discharge pressure.

problems.title: Heavy 2½ Smooth Bore Attack Line
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a 250 ft 2½-inch attack line with a 1⅛-inch smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 GPM at 50 PSI. The nozzle is one floor above the pump; use height = (floor number - 1) × 10 ft and elevation pressure = height × 0.5 PSI/ft. Use C = 2 and no appliance loss. What pump discharge pressure should be set?
formulaBreakdown: FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds of feet.
problems.instructorExplanation: This heavy-line variation uses 250 ft of 2½-inch hose at 265 GPM. Calculate friction loss with C = 2, then add nozzle pressure and elevation.
problems.explainMistake: Do not use the same answer for every 2½-inch line problem. Hose length and elevation can change the final pump discharge pressure.
